body.text: This note is written as a source document for formalization. All groups are written multiplicatively unless an abelian group is explicitly written additively.

body.label: Definition 1
body.definition:
**Definition 1 (Pseudo-length function).**
Let G be a group with identity element e. A function l : G -> R is a pseudo-length function on G if the following four conditions hold.
1. Nonnegativity: for every g in G, 0 <= l(g).
2. Identity: l(e) = 0.
3. Symmetry: for every g in G, l(g^{-1}) = l(g).
4. Triangle inequality: for every g,h in G, l(gh) <= l(g) + l(h).
body.name: PseudoLength

body.text: The nonnegativity condition is included explicitly so that the codomain can be the usual real numbers in a formalization. Equivalently, one may take the codomain to be the set of nonnegative real numbers and omit the separate nonnegativity axiom.

body.label: Definition 2
body.definition:
**Definition 2 (Length function).**
Let G be a group with identity element e. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is a length function if, for every g in G, g != e implies 0 < l(g).
body.name: IsLength

body.label: Definition 3
body.definition:
**Definition 3 (Homogeneous pseudo-length function).**
Let G be a group. A pseudo-length function l : G -> R is homogeneous if, for every g in G and every integer n in Z, one has l(g^n) = |n| l(g).
body.name: IsHomogeneousPseudoLength

body.text: In particular, for every g in G, homogeneity gives l(g^2)=2l(g).

body.label: Definition 4
body.definition:
**Definition 4 (Commutator).**
Let G be a group. For elements x,y in G, define the commutator [x,y] := xyx^{-1}y^{-1}.
body.name: commutator

body.label: Definition 8
body.definition:
**Definition 8 (Torsion-free abelian group).**
An abelian group A is torsion-free if T(A)={0}.
body.name: IsTorsionFreeAbelian

deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied to the concave function u ↦ sqrt(u) and the nonnegative random variable S_{2n}^2.

Each epsilon_i has mean 0 and variance 1.

deduced_from_theorem.description: Applied to the sum of 2n independent Rademacher random variables, each with variance 1.

proof_steps.proof_method: Divide the previous inequality by the positive denominator `m n` and use the definitions of `normQ(u + v)`, `normQ(u)`, and `normQ(v)`.

proof_steps.proof_method: Combine the verified seminorm axioms.

deduced_from_claim: normQ(a / n) = p(a) / n
proof_steps.claim: normQ(a tensor 1) = p(a) for every a in A.
proof_steps.proof_method: Use the representative `a tensor 1 = a / 1` in `VQ`.

proof.text: Since A is torsion-free, A injects into VQ and is identified with its image. Every element of VQ can be written as a/n with a in A and n positive. Define normQ(a/n)=p(a)/n. If a/n=b/m, then ma=nb in A, and homogeneity gives m p(a)=p(ma)=p(nb)=n p(b), so p(a)/n=p(b)/m; hence the definition is well-defined. Nonnegativity and normQ(0)=0 follow from p. Rational homogeneity follows by writing q=r/s and using homogeneity of p. For the triangle inequality, write u=a/n and v=b/m. Then u+v=(ma+nb)/(mn), so p(ma+nb) <= p(ma)+p(nb)=m p(a)+n p(b). Dividing by mn gives normQ(u+v) <= normQ(u)+normQ(v).

proof.text: By Lemma 9, l factors through a homogeneous pseudo-length function barL on G_ab. By Lemma 11, barL factors through the torsion-free quotient A:=G_ab/T(G_ab); let tildeL be the induced function. By Lemma 12, tildeL extends to a seminorm on VQ:=A tensor_Z Q. By Lemma 13, there exist a real Banach space B and a canonical seminorm-preserving map VQ -> B. Define phi as the composite G -> G_ab -> A -> VQ -> B. Each arrow is a group homomorphism into the additive group of B, so phi is a group homomorphism, and construction gives l(g)=||phi(g)||_B. If l is a length function and phi(g)=0, then l(g)=||phi(g)||_B=0, so g=e. Hence phi is injective.
